body_txt: Want an easy way to show that your character is loyal and tough? Make him a canine ! Want to come off as graceful while a bit of a loner? Make her a feline ! Want them to be tricky, cunning, or confident, or just knockout gorgeous? Choose a fox ! And while the stereotypes themselves vary from culture to culture, animals are one of the most popular forms of symbolism in fiction. When used as a characterization shortcut, the work compares the character to an animal for which the audience's culture projects certain personality traits. This can be done subtly with symbolism or Theme Naming , or more blatantly by giving the character physical traits or mannerisms of the animal, or even by making the animal itself the character. NOTE: For a general list, see Analysis.Animal Stereotypes for more information. As cultural stereotypes for animals vary from place to place and some cultures have multiple stereotypes for certain animals, make sure that your example lists the character(s), the animal, the cultural stereotypes that animal is representing, and how the character(s) reflect those traits. Animal-specific stereotypes
